title: VA Dental Care Access
summary:
VA is working to improve dental care access for eligible Veterans and is looking for a new administrator to operate the next generation of community care dental providers.

The goal is to build a stronger dental network with general and specialty care, preventive services, and pharmacy support.

Currently, about 26% of Veterans enrolled in VA health care are eligible for dental care.

To learn more, visit https://news.va.gov/press-room/va-moves-to-improve-dental-care-access-for-eligible-veterans/

— 
 
Explore VA.gov to learn about your benefits. 
 
Print out your VA Welcome Kit: https://www.va.gov/welcome-kit/ 
 
Whether you’re just getting out of the service or you’ve been a civilian for years now, the VA Welcome Kit can help guide you to the benefits and services you’ve earned. Based on where you are in life, your VA benefits and services can support you in different ways. 
 
Sign up for the #VetResources email newsletter: https://news.va.gov/vetresources/ 
 
#VetResources features Veteran-centric news stories, various deals and discounts, employment opportunities, and much more. 
 
#Veterans #VA #VABenefits #Healthcare

title: Aging Strong with VA: Community Residential Care (CRC)
summary:
In this episode of Aging Strong with VA, Deputy Secretary Paul R. Lawrence, PhD, discusses Community Residential Care (CRC). A program for Veterans who don’t need hospital or nursing home care but still need support, CRC offers VA-approved housing options across the country. Veterans can choose from assisted living, personal care homes, family care homes, and group living homes. These homes are inspected to ensure safety, quality, and dignity.

He'll walk you through how CRC helps Veterans maintain independence while receiving the care they need.

Watch the episode below.

title: Introduction to the Strength at Home app
summary: Strength at Home is a trauma-informed group program for those who struggle with conflict and anger in relationships and may be at risk for intimate partner violence. The Strength at Home mobile app is a free and publicly available app for people participating in the Strength at Home program. It was created by a team at the National Center for PTSD, Department of Veterans Affairs. The app can help you manage your sessions, handouts, and assignments; give you tools to help relax; and track your progress. This app is not intended for self-care. #strengthathome #intimatepartnerviolence #ipv

title: Introduction to the PTSD Coach app
summary: PTSD Coach is a free and publicly available mobile app for people with Posttraumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) and those interested in learning more about PTSD. It was created by a team at the National Center for PTSD, Department of Veterans Affairs. This app provides users with education about PTSD, information about professional care, a self-assessment for PTSD, opportunities to find support, and tools that can help users manage the stresses of daily life with PTSD. Tools range from relaxation skills and positive self-talk to anger management and other common self-help strategies. Users can customize tools based on their preferences and integrate their own contacts, photos, and music. This app can be used by people who are in treatment as well as those who are not.

title: The Whole Health Experience | Veterans Share Their Journey With VA Whole Health
summary:
What happens when healthcare stops asking "what's wrong with you" and starts asking "what matters to you?" That's the Whole Health Experience — VA's approach to care that meets Veterans where they are and helps them rediscover the life they want to live.

For many Veterans, healthcare can feel like it's about checking boxes. With Whole Health, it's an experience where VA health care is built around you — your story, your values, and what gives your life meaning — and for the Veterans in this video, it changed everything. 

For these Veterans, it meant more than managing symptoms — it meant rediscovering purpose, rebuilding strength, and taking charge of their own health. In this video, Ray, Lisa, Elena, and Kris share their personal stories of transformation through Whole Health — a holistic approach to care that goes beyond traditional medicine to ask what matters most to you.   

Whole Health supports your health and well-being. Whole Health centers around what matters to you, not what is the matter with you. This means your health team will get to know you as a person, before working with you to develop a personalized health plan based on your values, needs, and goals.

Experience Whole Health today at https://www.va.gov/wholehealth/experience or find a local Whole Health Point of Contact to get started. https://www.va.gov/WHOLEHEALTH/whole-health-facility-contacts.asp

#WholeHealth #VA #Healthcare

title: Most Californians Expect Little Change to Their Finances Despite Economic Uncertainty
summary: An overwhelming majority of Californians see bad financial times ahead and don’t think their income is keeping up with inflation. Still, most expect their own finances to hold steady—for now.

title: Unmasking Terrorism: The Truth Behind Recent Attacks
summary:
The conversation covers the misrepresentation of events by the media, the impact of immigration policies on terrorist attacks, the clarity of law enforcement response, and the importance of individual preparedness in the face of threats. It emphasizes the need for accurate reporting, effective law enforcement, and personal vigilance in the current security landscape.

Takeaways

  • Media Misrepresentation
  • Law Enforcement Response
  • Individual Preparedness

title: From San Bernardino to Foiled SF Plots: The Real Work of the Joint Terrorism Task Force
summary:
In this eye-opening episode of Your LEO Nation, hosted by retired California Highway Patrol Chief Mark Garrett, we sit down with special guest Nelson Duarte for an in-depth discussion on the Joint Terrorism Task Force (JTTF)—its structure, nationwide operations, and critical role in safeguarding communities.

Nelson Duarte shares firsthand insights from his experience, particularly highlighting the California Highway Patrol's (CHP) unique contributions to JTTF efforts in preventing and investigating terrorist threats. The conversation uncovers the often-unseen work of dedicated law enforcement professionals who serve as the first line of defense against terrorism.

Key highlights include:

  • Detailed breakdown of JTTF organization and collaboration across federal, state, and local agencies
  • CHP's respected position and integration within JTTF teams
  • In-depth look at the 2015 San Bernardino terrorist attack and how JTTF partners responded and investigated
  • The Everitt Aaron Jameson case (often referred to as Everett Jamerson) in San Francisco—a foiled ISIS-inspired plot targeting Pier 39 on Christmas Day
  • The vital, behind-the-scenes efforts of unsung heroes who thwart attacks before they happen
  • Ongoing terrorism threats, ideological battles, leadership's influence on law enforcement morale and effectiveness, the open border crisis, and the broader discussion around perceived threats like white supremacy

This episode shines a light on the real-world challenges, successes, and sacrifices of those protecting our nation—often without the recognition they deserve. Gain a deeper appreciation for the tireless work that keeps us safe and why public support and awareness matter more than ever.

Timestamps / Chapters:

  • 00:00 Introduction to JTTF and Guest Introduction
  • 05:19 JTTF Structure in California and Across the Country
  • 10:49 Role of CHP in JTTF and Perception Among Other Agencies
  • 17:01 San Bernardino Terrorist Attack and JTTF's Role
  • 27:26 Case of Everett Jamerson in San Francisco
  • 32:51 Preventing Terrorist Attacks
  • 38:20 Prevention and Ongoing Threats
  • 44:38 Impact of Leadership on Law Enforcement
  • 50:52 Open Border Crisis

If you're passionate about law enforcement, national security, or simply want to hear authentic stories from the front lines, this is a must-watch. Like, comment, subscribe, and hit the bell for more episodes of Your LEO Nation—where we honor the men and women who serve and protect.

#LawEnforcement #JTTF #TerrorismPrevention #CaliforniaHighwayPatrol #SanBernardino #PublicSafety #YourLEONation #MarkGarrett #NelsonDuarte

Support the show and stay informed—subscribe now!

title: More than words: Patrick Henry and the fight for freedom
summary:
On March 23, 1775, Patrick Henry stood inside St. John’s Church in Richmond and made his case for independence. British troops were occupying Boston. The king closed the ports and cut off trade. This meant merchants lost income, workers lost jobs, and families felt the strain. When colonists asked for relief, the king wouldn’t listen. […]

title: Chronic Absenteeism in California
summary: Missing school for 18 days or more is linked to academic underachievement, and any student absences affect districts, as school funding is based on attendance. Statewide, chronic absenteeism has dropped from its peak but remains well above rates before the pandemic; furthermore, improvement has slowed in the last year.

source: The Official United States Marine Corps Public Website News
title: From MRAP to MADIS: Legacy of Innovation Endures at NIWC Atlantic
summary: Throughout 2025, NIWC Atlantic delivered the MADIS drone-defense system to the Marine Corps. Leveraging lessons from the MRAP era, engineers used rapid prototyping and modular design to integrate advanced weapons and sensors onto JLTVs. This streamlined approach accelerates the fielding of lethal, connected technology to the modern warfighter.

title: U.S. SPACE COMMAND RECOGNITION
summary: COLORADO SPRINGS, CO — Two Marines from Marine Corps Forces Space Command (MARFORSPACE) were honored at the U.S. Space Command’s annual Apollo Awards during a ceremony held on February 19, 2026. Maj. Austin J. Oubre and Sgt. James Zsoka were recognized for their contributions to space operations, technology development, and their direct support to U.S. Space Command (USSPACECOM).

title: Lance Cpl. Schule Designs 3D Printed Antenna Mast, Saves Fleet Marine Force Thousands
summary: MARINE CORPS BASE CAMP LEJEUNE, N.C. — A U.S. Marine places a broken Mobile User Object System (MOUS) on a shelf, the fiberglass of the MOUS antenna mast brittle and weakened from use and exposure. It sits next to dozens of others, all unusable because of one flaw.

title: Operation Epic Fury: Why Trump’s Iran Strikes Don’t Need Congress
summary:
Operation Epic Fury: Why Trump’s Iran Strikes Don’t Need Congress

Is it inconsistent for Democrats to label President Trump a dictator over “Operation Epic Fury” against Iran while ignoring U.S. history?

Have presidents of both parties not led multiple undeclared wars and military actions, and does Article II not grant the president authority to initiate and direct military operations?

Is the 1973 War Powers Act unnecessary and unconstitutional, especially in light of Nancy Pelosi’s statement that President Obama did not need congressional authorization to bomb Libya?

Has the United States not effectively been at war with Iran since 1979, with Iran and its proxies killing at least 1,000 Americans?

Does waiting not risk a catastrophic nuclear attack, and did President Trump not act to protect Americans and U.S. allies, including Israel?

title: Have Racial Disparities in Law Enforcement Stops Narrowed?
summary: California’s Racial and Identity Profiling Act (RIPA) requires detailed reporting on all pedestrian and traffic stops. Recent RIPA data from the state’s largest law enforcement agencies points to a reduction in the overall number of stops—but we do not see a substantial narrowing of racial/ethnic disparities in intrusive experiences during stops.
